more.title: Mettler takes Tshwane to court over suspension vote
more.description: The legal battle over Tshwane City Manager Johann Mettler’s suspension has shifted from the council chamber to court, with his lawyers challenging the legality of the decision that removed the municipality’s accounting officer from office.Mettler, through Solidarity, has placed the suspension resolution under judicial scrutiny, arguing that the city failed to meet the legal requirements before taking the drastic step to suspend him.The challenge comes after the Tshwane council resolved on July 9 to place Mettler on precautionary suspension pending an investigation into allegations of...

more.title: Springboks overcome spirited Scotland to extend unbeaten run
more.description: The Springboks were pushed all the way by a fearless Scotland side on Saturday afternoon, but the world champions once again showed why they remain rugby’s benchmark, pulling away late to secure an entertaining 42-28 Nations Championship victory at Loftus Versfeld, Cape {town} Etc reports. In front of a crowd of more than 45,000 supporters in Pretoria, South Africa had... more.title: Cape Town retains title of South Africa’s highly financially sustainable metro
more.description: Cape Town has once again distinguished itself as the only metropolitan municipality in South Africa designated as ‘highly financially sustainable’, according to the latest Municipal Financial Sustainability Index (MFSI) unveiled by Ratings Afrika on 9 July. The recognition arrives amidst a challenging backdrop for many other municipalities facing significant financial strains. Mayor Geordin Hill-Lewis hailed this accolade, asserting that it...
